judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 1 correctly identifies that scoring/scorer.py re-appears in the main-diff as duplicated weighted-scorer content (matching expectation c), while Report 2 incorrectly concludes the diff is already clean and entirely pairwise work, missing that key finding — making Report 1 the stronger, more accurate report overall.

report_1.evidence: `main`. project documentation is explicit: "Base every PR on the trunk," ... Targeting `main` is now both correct per the rule and the only viable option, since the original base branch's commits are stale duplicates of what's already on trunk.

report_2.evidence: `main`. `feat/pairwise-metric` was built directly on top of `feat/weighted-scorer` (not on `main`), but `feat/weighted-scorer` has since been landed on `main`... So `main` is the only branch that makes sense as the PR target.

report_1.evidence: `feat/pairwise-metric` was branched from the tip of `feat/weighted-scorer` (`ac9d505`), not from `main` directly... `feat/weighted-scorer` is **not** a git ancestor of current `main`... yet its content is already on `main`... `feat/weighted-scorer` was squash-merged into `main` under a different commit/PR (#42)

report_2.evidence: So `feat/pairwise-metric` was branched from the tip of `feat/weighted-scorer`, not from `main`. That starting point (`feat/weighted-scorer`) has already been merged into `main`, but as a **squash merge**... none of `d6fdbc3`/`2d3029b`/`ac9d505` appear in `main`'s ancestry

report_1.evidence: `scoring/scorer.py`: the whole diff (docstring rename, `WEIGHTS` dict, weighted `score()` refactor) is **identical, line for line**, to the change already merged into `main` via commit `1971f08`. None of it is pairwise work — it's inherited from `feat/weighted-scorer`... It shows up in the diff only because `feat/pairwise-metric` still has `feat/weighted-scorer`'s now-obsolete commits underneath its own

report_2.evidence: That's it — 2 files, 5 insertions, 0 deletions. **Yes, this entire diff is pairwise-metric work.** No weighted-scorer content appears in the diff because `feat/weighted-scorer`'s changes are already present in `main`... so they net out to zero when diffed.

report_1.evidence: **Rebase `feat/pairwise-metric` onto current `origin/main`**... **Re-run `git diff origin/main...feat/pairwise-metric`** after the rebase and confirm it now shows only the `pairwise_preference()` function and the grader-prompt line — i.e., pure pairwise-metric work, nothing from the weighted-scorer dependency.

report_2.evidence: **Rebase `feat/pairwise-metric` onto `main`** (`git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric`... **Verify** post-rebase: confirm `git diff main feat/pairwise-metric` still shows exactly the same 2-file/5-line diff, confirm tests/build still pass, and confirm `git log --oneline main..feat/pairwise-metric` now shows only the 2 pairwise commits.
